[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Alto+saxophone%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Alto+saxophone%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Alto+saxophone%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Alto+saxophone%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is a member of the [saxophone](/wiki/Saxophone "Saxophone") family of [woodwind](/wiki/Woodwind "Woodwind") instruments.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Saxophones were invented by [Belgian](/wiki/Belgians "Belgians") instrument designer [Adolphe Sax](/wiki/Adolphe_Sax "Adolphe Sax") in the 1840s and patented in 1846. The alto saxophone is pitched in the key of [E♭](/wiki/E%E2%99%AD_(musical_note) "E♭ (musical note)"), smaller than the [B♭](/wiki/B%E2%99%AD_(musical_note) "B♭ (musical note)") [tenor](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone") but larger than the B♭ [soprano](/wiki/Soprano_saxophone "Soprano saxophone"). It is the most common saxophone and is used in [popular music](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music"), [concert bands](/wiki/Concert_band "Concert band"), [chamber music](/wiki/Chamber_music "Chamber music"), [solo repertoire](/wiki/List_of_concert_works_for_saxophone "List of concert works for saxophone"), [military bands](/wiki/Military_band "Military band"), [marching bands](/wiki/Marching_band "Marching band"), [pep bands](/wiki/Pep_band "Pep band"), [carnatic music](/wiki/Carnatic_music "Carnatic music"), and [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") (such as [big bands](/wiki/Big_band "Big band"), [jazz combos](/wiki/Jazz_combo "Jazz combo"), [swing music](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music")).

first_paragraph: The **alto saxophone** is a member of the [saxophone](/wiki/Saxophone "Saxophone") family of [woodwind](/wiki/Woodwind "Woodwind") instruments.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Saxophones were invented by [Belgian](/wiki/Belgians "Belgians") instrument designer [Adolphe Sax](/wiki/Adolphe_Sax "Adolphe Sax") in the 1840s and patented in 1846. The alto saxophone is pitched in the key of [E♭](/wiki/E%E2%99%AD_(musical_note) "E♭ (musical note)"), smaller than the [B♭](/wiki/B%E2%99%AD_(musical_note) "B♭ (musical note)") [tenor](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone") but larger than the B♭ [soprano](/wiki/Soprano_saxophone "Soprano saxophone"). It is the most common saxophone and is used in [popular music](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music"), [concert bands](/wiki/Concert_band "Concert band"), [chamber music](/wiki/Chamber_music "Chamber music"), [solo repertoire](/wiki/List_of_concert_works_for_saxophone "List of concert works for saxophone"), [military bands](/wiki/Military_band "Military band"), [marching bands](/wiki/Marching_band "Marching band"), [pep bands](/wiki/Pep_band "Pep band"), [carnatic music](/wiki/Carnatic_music "Carnatic music"), and [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") (such as [big bands](/wiki/Big_band "Big band"), [jazz combos](/wiki/Jazz_combo "Jazz combo"), [swing music](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music")).

The **alto saxophone** is a member of the [saxophone](/wiki/Saxophone "Saxophone") family of [woodwind](/wiki/Woodwind "Woodwind") instruments.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Saxophones were invented by [Belgian](/wiki/Belgians "Belgians") instrument designer [Adolphe Sax](/wiki/Adolphe_Sax "Adolphe Sax") in the 1840s and patented in 1846. The alto saxophone is pitched in the key of [E♭](/wiki/E%E2%99%AD_(musical_note) "E♭ (musical note)"), smaller than the [B♭](/wiki/B%E2%99%AD_(musical_note) "B♭ (musical note)") [tenor](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone") but larger than the B♭ [soprano](/wiki/Soprano_saxophone "Soprano saxophone"). It is the most common saxophone and is used in [popular music](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music"), [concert bands](/wiki/Concert_band "Concert band"), [chamber music](/wiki/Chamber_music "Chamber music"), [solo repertoire](/wiki/List_of_concert_works_for_saxophone "List of concert works for saxophone"), [military bands](/wiki/Military_band "Military band"), [marching bands](/wiki/Marching_band "Marching band"), [pep bands](/wiki/Pep_band "Pep band"), [carnatic music](/wiki/Carnatic_music "Carnatic music"), and [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") (such as [big bands](/wiki/Big_band "Big band"), [jazz combos](/wiki/Jazz_combo "Jazz combo"), [swing music](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music")).

The alto saxophone had a prominent role in the development of jazz. Influential jazz musicians who made significant contributions include [Don Redman](/wiki/Don_Redman "Don Redman"), [Jimmy Dorsey](/wiki/Jimmy_Dorsey "Jimmy Dorsey"), [Johnny Hodges](/wiki/Johnny_Hodges "Johnny Hodges"), [Benny Carter](/wiki/Benny_Carter "Benny Carter"), [Charlie Parker](/wiki/Charlie_Parker "Charlie Parker"), [Sonny Stitt](/wiki/Sonny_Stitt "Sonny Stitt"), [Lee Konitz](/wiki/Lee_Konitz "Lee Konitz"), [Jackie McLean](/wiki/Jackie_McLean "Jackie McLean"), [Phil Woods](/wiki/Phil_Woods "Phil Woods"), [Art Pepper](/wiki/Art_Pepper "Art Pepper"), [Paul Desmond](/wiki/Paul_Desmond "Paul Desmond"), and [Cannonball Adderley](/wiki/Cannonball_Adderley "Cannonball Adderley").

Although the role of the alto saxophone in [orchestral music](/wiki/Orchestra "Orchestra") has been limited, influential performers include [Marcel Mule](/wiki/Marcel_Mule "Marcel Mule"), [Sigurd Raschèr](/wiki/Sigurd_Rasch%C3%A8r "Sigurd Raschèr"), [Jean-Marie Londeix](/wiki/Jean-Marie_Londeix "Jean-Marie Londeix"), [Eugene Rousseau](/wiki/Eugene_Rousseau_(saxophonist) "Eugene Rousseau (saxophonist)"), and [Frederick L. Hemke](/wiki/Frederick_L._Hemke "Frederick L. Hemke").

## Range

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alto_saxophone&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Range")]

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg/60px-Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg.png)

[Chromatic scale](/wiki/File:Chromatic.ogg "File:Chromatic.ogg")

Full range (from B♭ to F, or from concert D♭ to concert A♭) chromatic scale

---

*Problems playing this file? See [media help](/wiki/Help:Media "Help:Media").*

As with most saxophones, the alto's written range is B♭3 to F♯6,[[2]](#cite_note-2) with the higher [altissimo](/wiki/Altissimo "Altissimo") register starting at G6. The written range for some student alto saxophones, like the [Yamaha](/wiki/Yamaha_Corporation "Yamaha Corporation") YAS-26, does not include a high F#, but the note can still be played using false fingerings. In concert (in the key of C) terms, this is from D♭to A♭. The saxophone's altissimo register is more difficult to control than that of other woodwinds and is usually only expected from advanced players. There is no well-defined upper limit for the altissimo register, but most fingering charts go up to a written D8 (sounding F7).

The alto saxophone is a [transposing instrument](/wiki/Transposing_instrument "Transposing instrument"), with pitches sounding a major sixth lower than written. In terms of concert pitches, the alto saxophone's range is from concert D♭3 (the D♭ below [middle C](/wiki/Middle_C "Middle C")—see [Scientific pitch notation](/wiki/Scientific_pitch_notation "Scientific pitch notation")) to concert A♭5 (or A5 on altos with a high F♯ key).

A few rare alto saxophones, like some [Selmer Mark VI](/wiki/Selmer_Mark_VI "Selmer Mark VI") models[[3]](#cite_note-3) and some made by Couesnon,[[4]](#cite_note-4) have been keyed to reach a low A, a semitone lower, similar to most [baritone saxophones](/wiki/Baritone_saxophone "Baritone saxophone").

## Alto saxophonists

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alto_saxophone&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Alto saxophonists")]

Main article: [List of saxophonists](/wiki/List_of_saxophonists "List of saxophonists")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Ornette-Coleman-2008-Heidelberg-schindelbeck.jpg/250px-Ornette-Coleman-2008-Heidelberg-schindelbeck.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ornette-Coleman-2008-Heidelberg-schindelbeck.jpg)

[Free jazz](/wiki/Free_jazz "Free jazz") pioneer [Ornette Coleman](/wiki/Ornette_Coleman "Ornette Coleman") playing a Selmer alto saxophone with a low A key

Notable jazz alto saxophonists include [Charlie Parker](/wiki/Charlie_Parker "Charlie Parker"), [Cannonball Adderley](/wiki/Cannonball_Adderley "Cannonball Adderley"), [Johnny Hodges](/wiki/Johnny_Hodges "Johnny Hodges"), [Sonny Stitt](/wiki/Sonny_Stitt "Sonny Stitt"), [Paul Desmond](/wiki/Paul_Desmond "Paul Desmond"), [Benny Carter](/wiki/Benny_Carter "Benny Carter"), [Ornette Coleman](/wiki/Ornette_Coleman "Ornette Coleman"), [Lee Konitz](/wiki/Lee_Konitz "Lee Konitz"), [Bobby Watson](/wiki/Bobby_Watson_(American_musician) "Bobby Watson (American musician)"), [Eric Dolphy](/wiki/Eric_Dolphy "Eric Dolphy"), [Marshall Allen](/wiki/Marshall_Allen "Marshall Allen"), [Art Pepper](/wiki/Art_Pepper "Art Pepper"), [Julius Hemphill](/wiki/Julius_Hemphill "Julius Hemphill"), [Oliver Lake](/wiki/Oliver_Lake "Oliver Lake"), [Anthony Braxton](/wiki/Anthony_Braxton "Anthony Braxton"), [Henry Threadgill](/wiki/Henry_Threadgill "Henry Threadgill"), [Carlos Ward](/wiki/Carlos_Ward "Carlos Ward"), [David Sanborn](/wiki/David_Sanborn "David Sanborn"), [Dave Koz](/wiki/Dave_Koz "Dave Koz"), [Tom Scott](/wiki/Tom_Scott_(saxophonist) "Tom Scott (saxophonist)"), [Paquito D'Rivera](/wiki/Paquito_D%27Rivera "Paquito D'Rivera"), [John Zorn](/wiki/John_Zorn "John Zorn"), [Tim Berne](/wiki/Tim_Berne "Tim Berne"), [Steve Wilson](/wiki/Steve_Wilson_(jazz_musician) "Steve Wilson (jazz musician)"), [Steve Coleman](/wiki/Steve_Coleman "Steve Coleman"), [Greg Osby](/wiki/Greg_Osby "Greg Osby"), [Vincent Herring](/wiki/Vincent_Herring "Vincent Herring"), [Mark Gross](/wiki/Mark_Gross_(musician) "Mark Gross (musician)"), [Kenny Garrett](/wiki/Kenny_Garrett "Kenny Garrett") and [Jeff Coffin](/wiki/Jeff_Coffin "Jeff Coffin").

Notable classical alto saxophonists include [Tim McAllister](/wiki/Tim_McAllister "Tim McAllister"), [Jean-Yves Fourmeau](/wiki/Jean-Yves_Fourmeau "Jean-Yves Fourmeau"), [Lawrence Gwozdz](/wiki/Lawrence_Gwozdz "Lawrence Gwozdz"), [Donald Sinta](/wiki/Donald_Sinta "Donald Sinta"), [Harvey Pittel](/wiki/Harvey_Pittel "Harvey Pittel"), [Larry Teal](/wiki/Larry_Teal "Larry Teal"), [Kenneth Tse](/wiki/Kenneth_Tse "Kenneth Tse"), [Arno Bornkamp](/wiki/Arno_Bornkamp "Arno Bornkamp"), [Harry White](/wiki/Harry_White_(saxophonist) "Harry White (saxophonist)"), [Otis Murphy](/wiki/Otis_Murphy "Otis Murphy"), [Claude Delangle](/wiki/Claude_Delangle "Claude Delangle").

[Kadri Gopalnath](/wiki/Kadri_Gopalnath "Kadri Gopalnath") was the pioneer of [Carnatic music](/wiki/Carnatic_music "Carnatic music") for the instrument. [Rudresh Mahanthappa](/wiki/Rudresh_Mahanthappa "Rudresh Mahanthappa") combines elements of jazz and Carnatic music for the alto saxophone like in his song Janani.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Manufacturers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alto_saxophone&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Manufacturers")]

Companies that currently produce saxophones include [Buffet Crampon](/wiki/Buffet_Crampon "Buffet Crampon"), [KHS](/wiki/KHS_Musical_Instruments "KHS Musical Instruments")/[Jupiter](/wiki/Jupiter_Band_Instruments "Jupiter Band Instruments"), [Conn-Selmer](/wiki/Conn-Selmer "Conn-Selmer"), [Selmer Paris](/wiki/Henri_Selmer_Paris "Henri Selmer Paris"), [Yamaha](/wiki/Yamaha_Corporation "Yamaha Corporation"), [Leblanc](/wiki/Conn-Selmer "Conn-Selmer")/[Vito](/wiki/Vito_(Leblanc) "Vito (Leblanc)"), [Keilwerth](/wiki/Keilwerth "Keilwerth"), [Cannonball](/wiki/Cannonball_Musical_Instruments "Cannonball Musical Instruments"), and [Yanagisawa](/wiki/Yanagisawa_Wind_Instruments "Yanagisawa Wind Instruments").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3e/YAS-62.jpg/250px-YAS-62.jpg)](/wiki/File:YAS-62.jpg)

Yamaha YAS-62 alto saxophone

## Classical music repertoire

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alto_saxophone&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Classical music repertoire")]

The alto saxophone has a large classical solo repertoire that includes solos with [orchestra](/wiki/Orchestra "Orchestra"), [piano](/wiki/Piano "Piano"), and [wind symphony](/wiki/Concert_band "Concert band"). Two important solo compositions are [Jacques Ibert](/wiki/Jacques_Ibert "Jacques Ibert")'s "*[Concertino da Camera](/wiki/Concertino_da_Camera "Concertino da Camera")*" and [Alexander Glazunov](/wiki/Alexander_Glazunov "Alexander Glazunov")'s "*[Concerto in E Flat major](/wiki/Saxophone_Concerto_(Glazunov) "Saxophone Concerto (Glazunov)")*".

The alto saxophone is found in the standard instrumentation of [concert bands](/wiki/Concert_band "Concert band") and [saxophone quartets](/wiki/Saxophone_quartet "Saxophone quartet"). Alexander Glazunov composed his Saxophone Quartet in B-flat major in 1932.

The alto saxophone is sometimes used in orchestral music. Some of the compositions where it appears are listed below.

* [Georges Bizet](/wiki/Georges_Bizet "Georges Bizet") features it in the "Intermezzo" and "Minuet" from the second suite of music from *[L'Arlésienne](/wiki/L%27Arl%C3%A9sienne_(Bizet) "L'Arlésienne (Bizet)").*
* It was called for by [Richard Strauss](/wiki/Richard_Strauss "Richard Strauss") in his *[Sinfonia Domestica](/wiki/Sinfonia_Domestica "Sinfonia Domestica")*, which includes parts for four saxophones including an alto saxophone in F.
* [Dmitri Shostakovich](/wiki/Dmitri_Shostakovich "Dmitri Shostakovich") uses the alto in his *[Suite for Variety Orchestra](/wiki/Suite_for_Variety_Orchestra_(Shostakovich) "Suite for Variety Orchestra (Shostakovich)")* and it has a prominent solo in the "Waltz No. 2" section. He also includes it in his *[Suite No. 1](/wiki/Suite_for_Jazz_Orchestra_No._1_(Shostakovich) "Suite for Jazz Orchestra No. 1 (Shostakovich)")* and *[Suite No. 2](/wiki/Suite_for_Jazz_Orchestra_No._2_(Shostakovich) "Suite for Jazz Orchestra No. 2 (Shostakovich)").*
* [Maurice Ravel](/wiki/Maurice_Ravel "Maurice Ravel") uses the saxophone prominently in his orchestration of [Modest Moussorgsky](/wiki/Modest_Moussorgsky "Modest Moussorgsky")'s *[Pictures at an Exhibition](/wiki/Pictures_at_an_Exhibition "Pictures at an Exhibition")*, most notably as the soloist in "Il vecchio castello".
* [Alban Berg](/wiki/Alban_Berg "Alban Berg") uses the saxophone in his late orchestral works, most notably "[Der Wein](/wiki/Der_Wein "Der Wein")", *[Lulu](/wiki/Lulu_(opera) "Lulu (opera)")*, and the *[Violin Concerto](/wiki/Violin_Concerto_(Berg) "Violin Concerto (Berg)").*
* [Sergei Rachmaninoff](/wiki/Sergei_Rachmaninoff "Sergei Rachmaninoff") uses the saxophone in his *[Symphonic Dances](/wiki/Symphonic_Dances_(Rachmaninoff) "Symphonic Dances (Rachmaninoff)")* as a soloist in the first movement.
* [George Gershwin](/wiki/George_Gershwin "George Gershwin") includes it in a few pieces; such as *[Rhapsody in Blue](/wiki/Rhapsody_in_Blue "Rhapsody in Blue")* and *[An American in Paris](/wiki/An_American_in_Paris "An American in Paris").*
* [Pierre Boulez](/wiki/Pierre_Boulez "Pierre Boulez") wrote for 2 alto saxes in his composition *[Pli selon pli](/wiki/Pli_selon_pli "Pli selon pli")* ("Fold by Fold").
* [Benjamin Britten](/wiki/Benjamin_Britten "Benjamin Britten") calls for an alto in his *[Sinfonia da Requiem](/wiki/Sinfonia_da_Requiem "Sinfonia da Requiem")* and *[The Prince of the Pagodas](/wiki/The_Prince_of_the_Pagodas "The Prince of the Pagodas").*
* [Leonard Bernstein](/wiki/Leonard_Bernstein "Leonard Bernstein") includes an alto sax in his *Symphonic Dances from [West Side Story](/wiki/West_Side_Story "West Side Story").*
* [Vincent d'Indy](/wiki/Vincent_d%27Indy "Vincent d'Indy") enlists two altos in his opera *[Fervaal](/wiki/Fervaal "Fervaal").*
* [Darius Milhaud](/wiki/Darius_Milhaud "Darius Milhaud") writes for an alto in *[La Creation du Monde](/wiki/La_Creation_du_Monde "La Creation du Monde")*, and places it in the score where one would expect to see a viola.
* [Allan Pettersson](/wiki/Allan_Pettersson "Allan Pettersson") makes use of an alto in his *16th Symphony.*
* [Krzysztof Penderecki](/wiki/Krzysztof_Penderecki "Krzysztof Penderecki") scores for two altos in his opera *[The Devils of Loudon](/wiki/The_Devils_of_Loudon "The Devils of Loudon")* ("Die Teufel von Loudon").
* [Aram Khatchaturian](/wiki/Aram_Khatchaturian "Aram Khatchaturian") includes an alto in his ballet *[Gayane](/wiki/Gayane_(ballet) "Gayane (ballet)").*
* [Poul Ruders](/wiki/Poul_Ruders "Poul Ruders") includes a significant solo for the alto saxophone in his orchestral suite *Concerto in Pieces.*
* [Eric Coates](/wiki/Eric_Coates "Eric Coates") wrote for an alto in his 1936 *Saxo-Rhapsody*.
* [Claude Debussy](/wiki/Claude_Debussy "Claude Debussy") features an alto in his *Rhapsody for Saxophone and Orchestra*.
* [Paul Creston](/wiki/Paul_Creston "Paul Creston") wrote a concerto for the alto, a sonata with piano, and a rapsodie with organ. He also included it in a suite for saxophone quartet.
* [Ronald Binge](/wiki/Ronald_Binge "Ronald Binge") wrote a concerto for the alto saxophone in E-flat major (1956)
* [Alan Hovhaness](/wiki/Alan_Hovhaness "Alan Hovhaness") includes an alto in his ballet [Is There Survival](/w/index.php?title=Is_There_Survival&action=edit&redlink=1 "Is There Survival (page does not exist)") (also known as [King Vahaken](/w/index.php?title=King_Vahaken&action=edit&redlink=1 "King Vahaken (page does not exist)")) and as part of incidental music he wrote to accompany the play *[The Flowering Peach](/wiki/The_Flowering_Peach "The Flowering Peach")*.
* [Béla Bartók](/wiki/B%C3%A9la_Bart%C3%B3k "Béla Bartók") calls for an alto in his ballet [The Wooden Prince](/wiki/The_Wooden_Prince "The Wooden Prince") (as well as tenor and baritones saxes).
* [Michael Tippett](/wiki/Michael_Tippett "Michael Tippett") employs an alto in his 1989 opera [New Year](/wiki/New_Year "New Year") (as well as soprano, tenor and baritone saxes).
* [Tony Banks](/wiki/Tony_Banks_(musician) "Tony Banks (musician)") wrote an alto feature for the first track of his 2012 album *[Six Pieces for Orchestra](/wiki/Six_Pieces_for_Orchestra "Six Pieces for Orchestra")*.
* [Igor Stravinsky](/wiki/Igor_Stravinsky "Igor Stravinsky") calls for two altos in his [Ebony Concerto](/wiki/Ebony_Concerto_(Stravinsky) "Ebony Concerto (Stravinsky)") (as well as two tenors and a baritone).
